mapping.a_element: analytic continuation of replica twist correlators in a complex variable
mapping.b_element: analyticity/crossing of the scattering amplitude as a function of a complex Mandelstam variable

brief_justification: The only genuine overlap is the generic use of analyticity in a complex variable; the transferable machinery differs decisively — paper A computes timelike entanglement entropy by a replica/analytic-continuation saddle-point selecting complex bulk geodesics (smallest real length), whereas paper B runs a dispersive primal S-matrix bootstrap, i.e. a convex optimization bounding a threshold amplitude under crossing, locality and partial-wave unitarity. A saddle-point entropy computation and a bootstrap bounding/optimization problem are different governing processes, so no coherent role-for-role mapping holds.
